channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Moraes autoriza Bolsonaro a receber Michelle e filhos no hospital
item.description: Minister Alexandre de Moraes authorized former president Jair Bolsonaro, hospitalized at DF Star Hospital with suspected aspiration bronchopneumonia, to receive visits from Michelle Bolsonaro and his sons Flávio, Carlos, Jair Renan, Laura, and stepdaughter Letícia Firmo da Silva. The Military Police of the Federal District was ordered to provide 24-hour surveillance, with at least two agents at the room’s door, banning electronic devices except medical equipment. Bolsonaro is serving a 27-year-and-3-month sentence for leading an attempted coup.

item.title: Lula diz que assessor de Trump só poderá entrar no Brasil após fim das sanções contra Padilha
item.description: President Lula barred the entry of Darren Beattie, a Trump advisor, into Brazil after Beattie sought to visit imprisoned former president Jair Bolsonaro. Lula tied the decision to the U.S. visa sanctions imposed since August 2025 on Health Minister Alexandre Padilha and his family, stating the restriction will remain until the visas are restored. The move follows a prior block by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, who canceled the visit over concerns of foreign interference, as flagged by the Brazilian foreign ministry.

item.title: Lula diz que proibiu entrada de conselheiro de Trump no país
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced he barred entry to Darren Beattie, a consultant to Donald Trump, in retaliation for the U.S. blocking the visa of Brazilian Health Minister Alexandre Padilha and his family. Lula stated the ban will remain until Padilha’s, his wife’s, and his 10-year-old daughter’s visas are restored. Beattie, a critic of Lula and Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, had planned meetings in Brazil with Bolsonaro allies to discuss electoral systems and social media censorship.

item.title: Lula diz que proibiu entrada de conselheiro de Trump no país
item.description: President Lula said he barred Darren Beattie, a counselor to former U.S. President Donald Trump, from entering Brazil in retaliation for the U.S. revoking the visa of Health Minister Alexandre Padilha.

item.title: Caso Master: Segunda Turma do STF forma maioria para manter prisão de Vorcaro; entenda
item.description: The Second Chamber of Brazil's Supreme Federal Court (STF) has so far maintained the preventive detention of Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, with votes from Justices Luiz Fux and André Mendonça. The votes of Nunes Marques and Gilmar Mendes are still pending in the virtual trial, which runs until March 20. Justice Dias Toffoli recused himself due to alleged ties to Banco Master, citing 'foro íntimo' as the reason.

item.title: STF reconhece nacionalidade brasileira para adotados no exterior
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court ruled that children adopted abroad by Brazilian parents and registered at consulates are entitled to Brazilian nationality upon turning 18. The unanimous decision rejected lower court interpretations that required naturalization, affirming constitutional equality between biological and adopted children. The ruling will apply to all similar cases and includes a binding legal thesis for future judgments.

item.title: Lula diz que assessor do governo Trump só vai poder entrar no Brasil se Padilha puder entrar nos EUA
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that Darren Beattie, a senior advisor to Donald Trump on Brazil-related matters, will only be allowed to enter Brazil when Health Minister Alexandre Padilha is permitted to enter the United States, following the blocking of Padilha’s, his wife’s, and daughter’s U.S. visas. Lula said he is protecting Padilha by barring Beattie’s entry. The stance was reinforced when Justice Alexandre de Moraes reversed a prior decision, denying Beattie’s visit to jailed ex-president Jair Bolsonaro, citing potential improper interference in Brazil’s internal affairs and lack of prior diplomatic notification.

item.title: Quebra de sigilo de Lulinha será julgada no plenário físico, decide Gilmar
item.description: Minister Gilmar Mendes ruled that the bank secrecy break of Lulinha, son of President Lula, and others investigated by the INSS CPMI will be judged in the STF’s physical plenary, despite an earlier suspension by Minister Flávio Dino. The case involves corruption allegations tied to social security benefits.

item.title: Bolsonaro tem líquido nos pulmões por broncoaspiração, diz Flávio
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was diagnosed with fluid in the lungs due to bronchoaspiration after experiencing chills and vomiting, as reported by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. He was taken to DF Star Hospital in Brasília and is under medical supervision, including by his personal physician Cláudio Birolini, who confirmed fever and low blood oxygen levels. Bolsonaro remains detained at the 19th Military Police Battalion in DF since the 2018 stabbing attack and has faced recurring health issues.

item.title: FPBio diz que subsídio ao diesel é alternativa equivocada
item.description: FPBio criticized the federal government's diesel subsidy as an equivocal measure that increases Brazil's dependence on imported fossil fuels and deepens energy vulnerability. The government announced a federal tax reduction and a direct subsidy of R$ 0.32 per liter, totaling R$ 0.64 off diesel prices through December 2026, at a fiscal cost of R$ 30 billion. FPBio argues that expanding biodiesel use would be the strategic alternative to lower prices and reduce fossil fuel dependence.

item.title: Gilmar suspende julgamento sobre quebra de sigilos na CPMI do INSS
item.description: Minister Gilmar Mendes suspended the STF judgment on the breaking of secrecy approved by the INSS CPMI after requesting a spotlight, which removed the analysis from the virtual plenary and forced an in-person session. The decision leaves in limbo the validity of the requests, including the one benefiting Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula, while Minister Flávio Dino had previously annulled the breaking of secrecy due to lack of individualized justification. The vote will now depend on scheduling by the Court's president for an in-person session.

item.title: Campanha de Oscar não é uma eleição política, afirma Kleber Mendonça Filho
item.description: Director Kleber Mendonça Filho states that the Oscar campaign for 'O Agente Secreto' is not a political election but a series of natural conversations with people passionate about the film. He highlights meetings with Pixar staff and Lupita Nyong'o as meaningful exchanges among cinema lovers. Although he prepared a speech in case of victory, he considers it normal not to use it, given the strong competition this year.

item.title: Broncopneumonia: entenda doença que afeta Bolsonaro e seus riscos
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro was hospitalized due to bronchopneumonia, a lung infection that can be severe in older adults, particularly when it lacks classic symptoms like fever and cough, delaying diagnosis. He exhibited chills, vomiting, and decreased oxygenation, necessitating hospitalization. The condition is primarily caused by bacteria such as Streptococcus pneumoniae and can progress to serious illness in individuals over 60.

item.title: As apostas do PL na campanha contra Eduardo Paes
item.description: The PL aims to nationalize the Rio de Janeiro campaign by tying Eduardo Paes to Lula’s government and Douglas Ruas to Bolsonaro, leveraging the 2022 presidential election results in the state. The strategy includes using ballot pamphlets with presidential and gubernatorial candidates on the same ticket, emphasizing public safety, and securing support from 69 of 92 municipal mayors.

item.title: Xiaomi Tag: um equipamento que fala com seu celular, qualquer que seja ele
item.description: The Xiaomi Tag is a tracking device that communicates with any smartphone via Bluetooth, allowing parents to monitor children’s real-time location in open spaces and crowded events. It works regardless of the phone’s brand or model, offering an accessible and universal solution to child safety concerns.

item.title: O fim da ‘água barata’: 2026 pode redefinir segurança hídrica no Brasil
item.description: Brazil has entered the era of the end of 'cheap water,' with water availability becoming a strategic factor for economic competitiveness and operational resilience. Despite successful initiatives like Aquapolo Ambiental and Campinas' EPAR, water reuse remains below 2% of treated sewage — far below the global average of 11% — indicating a vast untapped potential in the circular economy. While technical barriers have been overcome internationally, progress in Brazil remains slow due to institutional and management factors.

item.title: Moraes autoriza Bolsonaro a receber visitas de familiares no hospital
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes authorized former President Jair Bolsonaro to receive family visits at the private DF Star Hospital in Brasília after he fell ill at the Papuda prison, where he is incarcerated for attempting a coup d'état. Bolsonaro was transferred to the private hospital due to a deterioration in his health condition.

item.title: STF tem maioria para manter prisão de Daniel Vorcaro
item.description: The Second Chamber of the STF has formed a majority to maintain the imprisonment of Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, with votes from André Mendonça, Luiz Fux and Nunes Marques. Only Gilmar Mendes' vote is pending to confirm the decision, as Dias Toffoli is recused due to suspicion. If upheld, Fabiano Zettel and Marilson Roseno will also remain detained.

item.title: O pedido de Gilmar sobre a suspensão da quebra de sigilo de Lulinha
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Gilmar Mendes has requested that a case involving the suspension of bank and fiscal secrecy breaches — including that of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula — be moved from virtual session to the Court’s in-person plenary. The case stems from a decision by Minister Flávio Dino, who granted an injunction blocking the CPMI do INSS’s mass approval of 87 secrecy breaches, arguing they lacked individualized legal scrutiny. The matter will now be decided by the full Court in a date yet to be set.

item.title: Trump pressiona republicanos para aprovar lei de integridade eleitoral antes das legislativas
item.description: President Donald Trump is pressuring congressional Republicans to pass an election integrity bill before the November legislative elections, requiring voters to prove citizenship and present photo ID to vote. The bill, already passed by the House, faces strong obstacles in the Senate due to insufficient Republican votes to overcome Democratic opposition. Trump claims the measure would restore trust in elections, echoing his unsubstantiated allegations of widespread fraud in the 2020 presidential election.

item.title: Supremo mantém Daniel Vorcaro preso
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the detention of Daniel Vorcaro, founder of Banco Master, with a 3-0 vote in the 2nd Chamber, rapporteur André Mendonça joined by Fux and Nunes Marques. The ruling maintains Vorcaro’s isolation in a federal penitentiary in Brasília, citing concrete threats, seized phones, and the ongoing threat of the group 'A Turma'. Justice Toffoli recused himself due to prior bias; votes from Gilmar Mendes and Toffoli (recused) remain pending.

item.title: STF tem maioria para manter Daniel Vorcaro preso
item.description: The Second Chamber of Brazil's Supreme Federal Court (STF) formed a majority to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro, controller of Banco Master, with rapporteur Justice André Mendonça voting for the measure's continuation, joined by Justices Luiz Fux and Kassio Nunes Marques.

item.title: 'As pessoas têm que aprender a tirar a bunda da cadeira e andar um pouco', diz Lula ao comentar sobre uso de Ozempic
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva commented on the use of the medication Ozempic, stressing that medical guidance is essential and criticizing reliance on injections for weight loss without lifestyle changes. He emphasized the importance of healthy eating and stated that people who cannot lose weight due to health reasons must learn to 'get off the chair and walk a bit,' highlighting that walking is beneficial for health.

item.title: Meu amor vai ficar bem, diz Michelle sobre Bolsonaro
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro reassured supporters that her husband, former president Jair Bolsonaro, will be fine after being transferred to DF Star Hospital in Brasília due to chills and vomiting while incarcerated at the Papuda penitentiary. She shared a religious message on social media, expressing faith in God’s control. Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, a pre-candidate for president, confirmed the health episode and asked for prayers.

item.title: Augusto Cury se coloca como possível pré-candidato à Presidência
item.description: Psychiatrist and self-help author Augusto Cury has positioned himself as a possible pre-candidate for the 2026 Brazilian presidency, stating he will only run if formally invited by a political party. He emphasized that his motivation is not personal ambition but a sacrifice to pacify the nation, linking his potential candidacy to promoting emotional health and overcoming societal hatred and division. Cury stressed he seeks a state-oriented politics, not party politics, and that his pre-candidacy depends on dialogue with parties about projects, not individuals or ideologies.

item.title: Resolução do CFM sobre uso da IA por médicos deve ser revogada e revisada
item.description: The CFM approved Resolution 2.454/2026 to regulate AI use by physicians, exploiting Brazil’s lack of a general AI law and the pending PL 2338/2023. The regulation has been criticized for being imprecise, overstepping the authority of other agencies like Anvisa and the Ministry of Health, lacking regulatory impact assessment and public consultation, thus violating principles of legality, legitimacy, and social participation. This reflects a pattern of arbitrary CFM action that generates legal uncertainty and controversy.

item.title: Gilmar leva julgamento sobre quebra de sigilos de Lulinha ao plenário do STF
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Gilmar Mendes requested a highlight to move the judgment on the breaking of secrecy of businessman Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), son of President Lula, from the virtual to the physical plenary, restarting the process. Prior to the request, rapporteur Justice Flávio Dino had voted to maintain the suspension of the secrecy breaks approved en bloc by the INSS CPI, arguing each request should be analyzed separately. The date for the physical plenary hearing remains to be set by the court president.

item.title: STF começa a decidir se Vorcaro deve seguir preso; entenda o que está em jogo
item.description: The STF begins ruling on whether Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, should remain imprisoned, with implications extending beyond his case to test support for Minister André Mendonça and the Court’s credibility. Key scenarios include maintaining the preventive detention, requesting a adjournment (vista), or a dissenting vote favoring house arrest with electronic monitoring. With Justice Toffoli recused, only four justices are voting, and just two votes are needed to overturn the current order.

item.title: Bolsonaro, trasladado al hospital tras “sufrir escalofríos y vómitos”, según su hijo Flávio
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, 70, was transferred from prison to a hospital in Brasília on Friday morning after experiencing chills and vomiting, according to his son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. Police confirmed the move for medical evaluation, noting Bolsonaro has ongoing health issues, including a recent hernia surgery in January. Flávio asked for prayers that the condition is not serious, reiterating his father’s name as a potential presidential candidate in 2026.

item.title: Gleisi compartilha vídeo que liga Flávio a “submundo do crime”
item.description: Institutions Minister Gleisi Hoffmann shared a video on social media accusing Senator Flávio Bolsonaro of ties to militias and organized crime in Rio de Janeiro, citing Federal Police operations, alleged appointments, and decorations given to militia-linked figures. Bolsonaro denied the claims, stating he had only met one of the individuals mentioned twice. The post comes as polls show Flávio and Lula statistically tied in a potential 2026 presidential runoff.

item.title: Exportações do agro têm em fevereiro melhor resultado para o mês da série
item.description: Brazilian agribusiness exports reached US$12.05 billion in February 2026, the highest monthly figure in the historical series, according to the Ministry of Agriculture. This performance highlights the sector's strengthening in international trade, driven by global demand and the competitiveness of Brazilian products.

item.title: Exportações do agro têm em fevereiro melhor resultado para o mês da série
item.description: Brazilian agribusiness exports reached $12.05 billion in February 2026, the highest monthly figure in the historical series, up 7.4% year-on-year driven by a 9% increase in export volume. Despite a 1.5% decline in average international prices, the agro trade surplus reached $10.5 billion, with China as the top destination (30.5%) and strong growth in soybean, animal protein, and Asian markets like Vietnam and India.

item.title: Mendonça reforça decisão de prisão de Vorcaro; Fux acompanha
item.description: Supreme Court Justice André Mendonça upheld his order to arrest Daniel Vorcaro, founder of Banco Master, with support from Justice Luiz Fux, resulting in a 2-0 vote in the 2nd Chamber. Vorcaro remains in maximum-security isolation at the Federal Penitentiary in Brasília, pending votes from Gilmar Mendes and Nunes Marques. Justice Dias Toffoli recused himself due to prior impartiality concerns in related Master Bank cases.

item.title: Exportações do agro têm em fevereiro melhor resultado para o mês da série histórica
item.description: Brazilian agribusiness exports reached US$12.05 billion in February 2026, the highest historical result for the month, up 7.4% from February 2025, driven by a 9% increase in exported volume. The agricultural trade surplus reached US$10.5 billion, with China as the top destination (30.5%), followed by the European Union and the United States. The soybean complex led exports, followed by animal protein, forest products, coffee, and the sugarcane ethanol complex.

item.title: André Mendonça e Luiz Fux votam para manter prisão preventiva de Vorcaro
item.description: Supreme Court ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the preventive detention of former banker Daniel Vorcaro, accused of financial fraud in the 'Compliance Zero' operation. The trial is being held by the Second Chamber of the STF, with only four votes due to Justice Dias Toffoli's recusal, which could result in a tie and, under internal rules, Vorcaro's release.

item.title: STF tem dois votos para manter prisão de Daniel Vorcaro
item.description: Justices André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the imprisonment of Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, and two others before the STF. The trial is underway in the Second Chamber, with votes from Gilmar Mendes and Nunes Marques still pending. In case of a tie, the position most favorable to the accused prevails, as Justice Dias Toffoli has recused himself.

item.title: Sentimento do consumidor nos EUA cai a 55,5 em março, mostra pesquisa preliminar
item.description: The University of Michigan's U.S. consumer sentiment index fell from 56.6 in February to 55.5 in March, according to preliminary data released Friday. The decline was slightly more than analysts had expected (55.3). Twelve-month inflation expectations remained steady at 3.4%, while the five-year outlook edged down from 3.3% to 3.2%.

item.title: PSD aciona STF contra lei que regulamenta eleição indireta para governador do Rio; entenda
item.description: The PSD filed a Direct Action of Unconstitutionality (ADI) with the STF against Rio de Janeiro’s law regulating the indirect election for governor, specifically challenging open voting and the 24-hour incompatibility deadline for candidates. The party argues these provisions violate the Federal Constitution and enable political power abuse, benefiting allies of Governor Cláudio Castro, such as Nicola Miccione and Douglas Ruas, as well as PT’s André Ceciliano. The law was signed hours before by Castro, who will resign in April to run for Senate, creating a power vacuum to be filled by a slate chosen by the 70 Alerj deputies through the end of 2026.

item.title: PF deve pedir mais prazo a Mendonça para concluir inquérito do Banco Master
item.description: The Federal Police is expected to request additional time from Supreme Court Justice André Mendonça to conclude the Banco Master investigation due to the large volume of unanalyzed data, including at least seven seized devices. The PF anticipates new phases of the operation and potentially new suspects, which is already causing concern within the STF over the possible implication of ministers.

item.title: Trama golpista: Primeira Turma do STF julga recursos contra a condenação de grupo que espalhava fake news
item.description: The First Chamber of the STF is reviewing appeals from individuals convicted for their role in the 2022 coup attempt, specifically for spreading false information about electronic voting machines and attacking institutions. Relator Justice Alexandre de Moraes voted to uphold the convictions of Nucleus 4, with sentences ranging from 7 to 17 years in prison. The virtual hearing continues until March 20, unless a request for view or certification interrupts it.

item.title: SUS passa a adotar antibiótico para prevenir sífilis e clamídia
item.description: The SUS has adopted doxycycline 100 mg as post-exposure prophylaxis to prevent syphilis and chlamydia, following approval by Conitec and publication in the Official Gazette. Technical areas have up to 180 days to implement the measure in health units, expanding access to prevention of bacterial STIs among sexually active populations.

item.title: Copa do Brasil define confrontos da 4ª fase; veja duelos e premiação
item.description: The third round of the 2026 Copa do Brasil concluded on Thursday (12), determining the 24 clubs advancing to the fourth round, which will decide the 12 spots in the next stage. The matchups for the fourth round have already been set and will take place soon, maintaining the single-elimination format. The tournament continues to offer substantial prize money to participating clubs, encouraging competitive performance.

item.title: Julgamento de Vorcaro: Mendonça e Fux defendem manutenção de preventiva
item.description: Supreme Court ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro in a virtual plenary session. Mendonça, the case's rapporteur, presented his vote on Friday morning, the 13th, and was followed by Fux. Ministers Kassio Nunes Marques and Gilmar Mendes have yet to vote.

item.title: Petrobras adere ao subsídio do governo para diesel
item.description: Petrobras has joined the federal government's diesel subsidy program authorized by MP 1.340/2026, which provides R$0.32 per liter in subsidies and a fiscal impact of R$10 billion for the Treasury. The adhesion is conditional on the ANP publishing operational rules and aims to mitigate international price volatility without passing costs to domestic consumers. The government states the full package, including tax cuts, will impact public accounts by R$30 billion by December 2026, in response to rising oil prices due to Middle East tensions involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran.

item.title: Gilmar Mendes leva para sessão presencial decisão sobre quebra de sigilo de ‘Lulinha’
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Gilmar Mendes requested a review (destaque) of the virtual deliberation on the annulment of data seizure orders targeting Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), transferring the case to an in-person session. Justice Flávio Dino had voted to uphold the annulment of the bulk-approved data seizures by the INSS Congressional Inquiry Committee, citing lack of individualized voting on the 87 requests. Although Dino’s decision prevents the use of already-obtained data in the committee’s final report, the seized information remains in the CPMI’s possession and will now be debated orally by all 11 justices.

item.title: Mendonça inicia julgamento no STF e vota para manter Vorcaro preso
item.description: Ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux of the STF voted to maintain the preventive detention of Daniel Vorcaro, former Banco Master banker, in a virtual trial of the Second Chamber. Vorcaro was re-arrested on March 4 after the Federal Police found messages on his phone indicating a plan to fake an assault against journalist Lauro Jardim to intimidate him. He is suspected of leading a private militia called 'A Turma' and had previously been arrested in November for attempting to flee the country.

item.title: Mendonça e Fux votam por manter a prisão de Vorcaro
item.description: Justices André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the imprisonment of Daniel Vorcaro, former president of Banco Master, in the Second Chamber of the STF, with Justice Dias Toffoli recused. The ruling pertains to Operation Compliance Zero, investigating organized crime, money laundering, and other offenses tied to the bank’s scheme. Mendonça stated his vote supported the precautionary measure, except for Luiz Phillipi Machado de Moraes Mourão, who died in custody.

item.title: André Mendonça e Fux votam para manter prisão preventiva de Vorcaro
item.description: Supreme Court ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the preventive detention of former banker Daniel Vorcaro of Banco Master in the 2nd Chamber. As rapporteur, Mendonça and Fux supported keeping Vorcaro, Fabiano Zettel, and Marilson Roseno da Silva in prison over alleged involvement in the Master scheme. The vote remains pending from Kássio Nunes Marques and Gilmar Mendes, while Dias Toffoli recused himself, raising the possibility of a tie that would favor Vorcaro under STF rules favoring the defendant in penal ties.

item.title: Caso Master: Mendonça e Fux votam para manter prisão de Vorcaro; placar está 2x0
item.description: Supreme Court Ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro in the third phase of Operation Compliance Zero, resulting in a 2-0 partial score. Gilmar Mendes and Nunes Marques have yet to vote, with a deadline until May 20. Mendonça argued that evidence from the first seized phone justifies the measure and rejected the defense’s claim that the 'A Turma' WhatsApp group was merely casual, citing the inclusion of a federal police officer and the organization’s latent threat.

item.title: Mendonça abre julgamento e vota para manter Vorcaro em presídio de segurança máxima; Fux acompanha
item.description: Minister André Mendonça initiated the vote at the Supreme Federal Court to keep banker Daniel Vorcaro in preventive detention at the Federal Penitentiary of Brasília, with Minister Luiz Fux supporting the motion, resulting in a 2-0 score. The trial also examines the continued detention of Vorcaro’s brother-in-law and an alleged leader of his private militia, 'A Turma'. The verdict is expected by next Friday, the 20th, pending votes from Gilmar Mendes and Nunes Marques.
